humans come up with rules and then convert these rules into a programming language like python and we talked about an example of spam prediction so we would manually in a rule-based system we would out uh like what are the patterns present in these features and what we can use to actually make a decision if something is a spam or not then we talked about this concept of supervised learning because both these examples of price prediction and spam prediction is supervised learning because we have the target variable so the y here is our target variable so this is the information we want to predict we know this and this is something we want to learn so our model g so this is a model so the model extracts patterns from data and then for data for which we don't know the price so this is deployment step because without this deployment step even the best model is not useful so and machine learning is just a part of the entire process we talked then in more details about this the modeling step and we talked about the process of selecting the best model and there we take the entire data set we split it into three parts and we use one part validation data set for finding the bus model and then we use another part training testing data set to make sure that we don't accidentally pick a model that got good results just by chance then uh lesson six wasn't uh really a lesson so interested it's also possible to actually create a server to run the server on aws or other cloud providers and use it as well then in lesson seven we talked about numpy so numpy is a library in python for manipulating numerical data so numerical arrays and we talked about different operations that are useful for data science and machine learning that are available in number then in the lesson eight we talked about linear algebra we talked about multiplication so we talked about uh vector vector multiplication so if you remember we had two vectors u and v and we talked about multiplication is possible to express as a bunch of vector vector multiplications and we saw that actually if you implement everything in code the formulas no longer look scary and then finally in
